text: An overview of the language situation in Cieszyn Silesia /1/ After a brief presentation of the background to the subject (the division of Cieszyn Silesia in 1920, the traditional multi-ethnic and multi-lingual nature of the region, Czech language policy, Polish minority institutions and clubs in the Czech part of the Zaolzie region), the author turns to the presentation of the current language situation in public space. She is particularly concerned with the functioning of the languages (based on written and spoken forms of formal and informal language) and focuses on the following issues: - recipients of the Polish language on the Czech bank of the border river Olza, - purposes for which the Czech language is used in the Polish part of the originally homogeneous city. The authenticity of the linguistic material is preserved. The author analyses this material from the following points of view: - language means: - - lexical borrowings from foreign non-Slavic languages and their declension, - - creation and use of diminutives, - - interlingual homonymy, - - graphical means: - - - form of letters, - - - spelling rules, - - pragmatics/cultural context of the texts. The author endeavours to answer the following questions: - how the ref lection of bilingualism in the overall appearance of both cities is presented and how it dif fers on the Polish and Czech sides.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
